{{Quote|I will command the ground forces in a frontal assault on the ruins of the capital city, Kesiak.|[[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]]|<ref name="Game">[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars (video game)|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' video game]]</ref>}}
'''Kesiak''', also known as '''Kesiak City''', was the old [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Thule/Legends|Thule]]. It was the stronghold of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] armies during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Sith War]], as well as the location of an ancient weapon, the [[Dark Reaper]].

==History==
Early into the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], remnants of Kesiak's aged, hulked cityscape and the surrounding area became the site of latter stages of a [[Dark Reaper crisis|campaign]] pitting the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Clone Army]] against [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] as the former [[Battle of Thule|fought]] to prevent the activation of the Dark Reaper. Since the Confederacy had maintained earlier control of the world, the [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|Droid Army]] was widely deployed among Kesiak's avenues, even operating tower turrets at various roadblocks.

[[File:Ruins_of_Kessia.jpg|thumb|left|[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], surrounded by the aftermath of the [[Battle of Thule]], which was fought primarily in Kesiak.]]
During the final stages of the battle, [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] successfully navigated the city service ducts, cutting down [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|various]] [[DSD1 dwarf spider droid/Legends|droids]] along the way, so as to eliminate the gate mechanism blocking off entry to Kesiak. [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] then led clone forces into the breakthrough of the defenses. The briefly activated Reaper was then challenged by the young Jedi and defeated.

Following the battle, Kesiak was left in smoldering ruins.
